name: A BETTER CREDIT TODAY CREDIT REPAIR
description_short: Miami-based women-owned credit repair firm founded in 2016, disputing negative items and providing credit counseling, education, and free consultations.
description_long:
A Better Credit Today Credit Repair was founded in 2016 and operates out of Miami, Florida (8950 SW 74th Ct, Suite 2201-A13). The business is women-owned and serves consumers across the South Florida market. No CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications have been publicly confirmed, and the company maintains a limited web presence, making independent verification of credentials difficult. Despite a small digital footprint, the company has accumulated a 5.0-star Google rating across 22 reviews, suggesting consistent client satisfaction among those who have used their services.

The company's core offering is credit repair: identifying and disputing negative, inaccurate, or unverifiable items on consumer credit reports. Beyond dispute work, A Better Credit Today offers credit counseling sessions and positions itself as an educational resource for consumers trying to understand and improve their credit profiles. They provide a free credit e-book and maintain a "credit hub" — an educational resource center — as part of their service. A free initial consultation is also offered, allowing prospective clients to assess their situation before committing. No pricing information is publicly available; all plan details must be obtained directly from the company.

The women-owned designation and the active military discount set A Better Credit Today apart from many competing credit repair shops in the Miami market. The company targets consumers who have been denied credit, are paying elevated interest rates due to damaged credit, or are simply looking for a structured path to credit score improvement. Their pairing of dispute services with educational materials suggests a philosophy of building client financial literacy alongside the repair work itself — a model associated with longer-term credit health outcomes.

Honestly, A Better Credit Today's very limited public web presence is a real constraint for prospective clients doing due diligence. No BBB profile, no publicly listed pricing, no confirmed website, and no verifiable certifications have been found in any indexed source as of March 2026. The 22 Google reviews and 5.0-star rating are encouraging but represent a small sample. Consumers considering this firm should contact them directly, ask explicitly about their dispute methodology, turnaround timelines, and any fees before signing. The company appears best suited for Miami-area consumers who prefer working with a small, personal firm and can meet in person or by phone rather than through a full-featured online portal.
pros: Women-owned small business — a differentiator in the credit repair industry
Military discount available for active-duty and veteran clients
Perfect 5.0-star Google rating across 22 reviews as of early 2026
Free initial consultation before any financial commitment
Offers free credit e-book and a dedicated credit education hub
Founded in 2016 with nearly a decade of operational experience in Miami
Local Miami presence allows for in-person consultations — rare among credit repair firms
cons: No publicly available pricing — costs are opaque until you contact the company directly
Very limited web presence; no confirmed website URL indexed as of March 2026
No BBB profile found, making third-party accreditation verification impossible
No certifications (NFCC, HUD, CDFI) verified — unclear if counselors hold formal credentials
Only 22 Google reviews — small sample size makes rating harder to rely on statistically
best_for: Miami-area consumers who prefer working with a local, in-person credit repair firm
Active military members and veterans seeking a credit repair provider with a military discount
Consumers who have been denied credit or are paying high interest rates due to negative items
Clients who want educational resources alongside dispute work, not just a dispute-filing service
diagnosis: A Better Credit Today is best for Miami-area consumers who want a small, locally operated credit repair firm with a personal touch — particularly military members who can take advantage of their discount. The main caveat is near-total pricing opacity and an absence of verifiable third-party credentials; prospective clients should request a full fee schedule and ask about dispute methodology before signing any agreement.
